item.title: Aid Groups Flock to Venezuela In Search-and-Rescue Frenzy
item.description: Two of the US military ships used in a blockade meant to pressure Nicolás Maduro have headed back toward Venezuela, this time with rescue teams, equipment and medical aid after devastating twin earthquakes struck the country this week.

item.title: Philippine Government Plans to Raise Budget by 6% Next Year
item.description: The Philippines plans to increase the budget by 6% next year to 7.2 trillion pesos ($117 billion), according to the budget department.

item.title: Bolivia Moves to Flexible Exchange-Rate System After 15 Years
item.description: Bolivia is moving to a flexible exchange-rate system to strengthen macroeconomic stability, its Finance Ministry announced Friday.

item.title: Atlanta Fed Chief Selection Delay Gives Warsh a Say
item.description: The Federal Reserve Bank of Atlanta has resumed its search for a new president following the arrival of Kevin Warsh, allowing the new Fed chairman to have a say in the selection of a key official who will vote on interest rates in 2027.

item.title: Anthropic’s Mythos 5 AI Model Cleared by US for Wider Use
item.description: The US eased restrictions on the use of Anthropic PBC’s powerful Mythos 5 artificial intelligence model for “trusted partners,” according to a letter from the Commerce Department.

item.title: Hellman & Friedman-Backed Hub Files Confidentially for IPO
item.description: Hub International Holdings Inc., an insurance broker backed by Hellman & Friedman, confidentially filed for an initial public offering whose proceeds could be used by the to pare its debt.

item.title: SpaceX, Charter Discussed Mobile Phone Partnership in US
item.description: SpaceX and Charter Communications Inc. have held executive-level talks about partnering on a consumer mobile phone offering, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: Biolife Is Said to Have Drawn Takeover Interest From Repligen
item.description: Biolife Solutions Inc. has attracted takeover interest from parties including diagnostics company Repligen Corp., according to people with knowledge of the matter.

item.title: Anthropic Moves Toward Deal With US to Lift Curbs on AI Models
item.description: Anthropic PBC and the Trump administration are moving closer to an agreement that would lift US restrictions on the company’s top two artificial intelligence models after weeks of talks between the two sides over security of the systems, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: S&P Keeps US Sovereign Rating At AA+ With Stable Outlook
item.description: S&P Global Ratings affirmed the US’s credit rating at AA+, one level below the top rank, citing a resilient economy and high but stable fiscal deficits.

item.title: キオクシア米上場計画、高市政権「370兆円投資」、投資のプロ4人が明かす1000万円投資術──1週間のニュース5選
item.description: マーケットで話題になったニュースから、1週間を振り返る五つのトピックを厳選して紹介します。

item.title: AI Rout Exposes Wall Street’s $270 Billion Speculation Machine
item.description: This week’s tech rout did more than puncture one of the year’s hottest trades: it exposed the machinery of modern speculation — and how quickly it can work in reverse.

item.title: An Analyst’s Missed Remark Surfaced in Deadly Iran School Strike Probe
item.description: An intelligence analyst’s missed remarks are among the missteps surfaced in an investigation into a deadly strike against an elementary school in Iran.

item.title: Magnolia Oil & Gas Is in Lead to Acquire WildFire for Over $4 Billion
item.description: Magnolia Oil & Gas Corp. has emerged as the front-runner to acquire closely held WildFire Energy for more than $4 billion in what would rank as its largest-ever acquisition, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: TPG, Leonard Green Explore Sale of McIlroy-Backed Troon Golf
item.description: The private equity owners of Troon Golf are exploring a potential sale of the company, which is one of the largest US golf course operators, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: Beyond Chips, IMF Sees AI Wealth Boom Adding to Inflation Risks
item.description: Artificial intelligence may fuel inflation not just by driving up the cost of chips, but also by making consumers wealthier and more willing to spend, according to the International Monetary Fund’s chief economist.

item.title: Apple’s Vision Pro and Smart Glasses Chief to Join OpenAI
item.description: Apple Inc.’s top executive in charge of the Vision Pro headset and the company’s smart glasses efforts is leaving for OpenAI, continuing a streak of high-profile defections to rivals in the artificial intelligence and hardware sectors.

item.title: CFTC Conducting Broad Probe Into Prediction Market Polymarket
item.description: The US Commodity Futures Trading Commission is conducting an extensive investigation into Polymarket, according to a person familiar with the matter, the latest challenge for the prediction market platform plagued by operational stumbles and legal scrutiny.

item.title: South Korean Regulators Are Lamenting Leveraged ETFs
item.description: Frenzied trading in South Korea has renewed concerns about the leveraged ETF boom

item.title: Uber Tightens US Driver Background Checks as Sexual Assault Cases Mount
item.description: Uber Technologies Inc. is tightening driver background checks in the US and applying the new standards retroactively to existing workers, the company’s latest response to a wave of sexual-assault lawsuits brought by passengers.

item.title: Exxon’s Top US Gas Trader to Join Expand as Exits Hit Oil Giant
item.description: Exxon Mobil Corp.’s head of US gas and power trading is moving to Expand Energy Corp., one of several recent departures from a unit the supermajor has been trying to grow.

item.title: What Is AI Distillation and Why Is It a Worry for the Industry?
item.description: Artificial intelligence companies in the US have spent hundreds of billions of dollars to develop more advanced chatbots, betting they can earn enough from customers to justify the investment. That approach carries the risk of being undermined by rivals who build competing AI systems for far less.

item.title: Stock Valuations Should Worry Investors, Abby Joseph Cohen Says
item.description: Lofty stock prices may be hiding risks investors should be paying more attention to, especially in the labor market, according to Abby Joseph Cohen, the former Goldman Sachs partner and now Columbia Business School professor.

item.title: Venezuela Quakes Did $6.7 Billion in Direct Damage, UN Estimates
item.description: Venezuela’s devastating earthquakes this week affected nearly two million buildings, causing $6.7 billion in direct damage, according to estimates by a United Nations body.

item.title: China Ousts Six Military Lawmakers as Xi’s Purge Continues
item.description: China stripped six military lawmakers of their seats in the national parliament in the latest sign that President Xi Jinping is continuing with a purge of key defense establishment personnel.

item.title: OpenAI Limits Release of New Model Under Pressure From US
item.description: OpenAI is rolling out a preview version of a more capable new artificial intelligence model to select partners before making it available more widely in the coming weeks, following pressure from the Trump administration to stagger the release.

item.title: Trump Vows 100% Tariff on Countries Over Digital Services Taxes
item.description: President Donald Trump threatened to impose 100% tariffs on goods imported from countries that impose digital services taxes, escalating pressure on European nations that just ratified a trade pact with the US.

item.title: Brookfield Is in Fight Over Solar Firm Where Cash ‘Evaporated’
item.description: Joh. Berenberg, Gossler & Co. KG and Brookfield Asset Management Ltd. are battling over the future of GoldenPeaks, an energy company staring at collapse after borrowing $1.5 billion for European solar-power projects.

item.title: BlackBerry CEO Says Safety Software Offers AI-Resistant Growth
item.description: BlackBerry Ltd. Chief Executive Officer John Giamatteo had a direct answer when asked about one of the biggest challenges to software providers: Artificial intelligence isn’t ready to displace his safety-certified products.

item.title: Crypto’s ETF Boom Gets $4.5 Billion Reality Check in Brutal Week
item.description: The investors who were supposed to bring stability to Bitcoin are heading for the exits.
